sprint: Magic Card Awakening — Seven-Item Bug Batch + No-Scrolls Fit Pass
task: Fixed seven real, user-reported bugs across Publish Studio and the Magic Card Awakening ceremony, then closed a separately-raised, explicit 'no scrolls on any screen' requirement across every screen this batch touched. (1) The ambient companion widget disappeared the instant Publish Studio opened on all three named stages (Read My Story / Almost Ready / Choose Story Destination) -- root cause: js/companionDirector.js's BUSY_SELECTORS treated '.publish-studio-modal:not(.hidden)' as just another busy dialog to hide behind, the same class of occlusion bug the Companion Canon Freeze sprint already found and fixed once for the Magic Card overlay itself. Fixed by removing it from BUSY_SELECTORS outright (a disclosed judgment call: Publish Studio has no single per-stage selector to scope this more narrowly, so the fix applies to all five of its stages, not just the three named in the report) -- verified visually across Read/Almost-Ready/Destination with the companion correctly present and never clashing with the modal's own layout. (2) The Awakening reveal's 'It's yours, if you'd like it.' constellation showed only bare dots with no star shape, and the claim panel that follows had no card visual at all -- js/magicCardUI.js's _renderConstellation() gained a new _sparklePoints() helper drawing an 8-point sparkle polygon behind each star's existing circle (a real star glyph, not a dot), and both the reveal and claim screens now wrap the constellation in a new .magic-card-sky-frame -- a real card-shaped frame (gold border, radial-gradient background matching MagicCardArt's own canvas gradient) instead of a bare SVG floating on the page. (3) The claim panel gave no reason a sky matters -- added a new .magic-card-claim-subtext line ('No two skies are the same -- yours is the one thing that can bring this card back to you, on any device, any time.') explaining the sky's real purpose (cross-device recall), not just naming it. (4) The nickname prompt ('What should we call you?') showed no preview of either card side, so a child couldn't see where their name would actually land -- rewritten to build a live, two-sided Front/Back preview via MagicCardArt.drawFront/drawBack against a lightweight, non-persisted draft-card object, with an input listener redrawing the Front live as the child types -- the exact real card layout, not a mockup. (5) The Story Egg's 'magic' pose showed a broken-image glyph mid-ceremony -- root cause: a pose declared in a companion.json states map (e.g. magic:'magic.png') was never a guarantee the file had actually been uploaded (a real, previously-disclosed gap for Story Egg's own hero/magic poses); the old code only fell back to the package's defaultState when a pose wasn't DECLARED at all, never when the declared file itself 404s. Fixed with a real onerror handler on the ceremony's that falls back to the package's own already-uploaded defaultState pose the instant the declared file fails to load -- the same graceful-degradation discipline CompanionEngine's own ambient widget already relies on, made real for this separate ceremony-stage rendering path too; confirmed via a full ceremony playthrough that the previously-broken 'magic' beat now silently and correctly shows the Story Egg's idle pose instead, with zero broken images across all 7 beats. (6) The First Claimed Moment's two-sided card view was the same small size as Magic Card Home's own card view, reading as an afterthought for what should be the ceremony's climactic moment -- scoped a new, larger .magic-card-art-canvas width (clamp(150px,32vw,240px)) specifically to .magic-card-awaken-stage's own art view, leaving Home's separate, unrelated card view at its original size. (7) Every fresh Story published 'The Worst Birthday Ever' as its title, a leftover placeholder joke that survived every real Creator's own book because Locked Product Decision #1 (Workspace Simplification) deliberately hides the Book Title field from the editor, so nothing ever overwrote it -- fixed at both real defaults, js/state.js's AppState.project.title/.bookTitle and index.html's hidden #bookTitle input, to a neutral 'My Adventure'. Mid-batch, a new, explicit, high-priority requirement arrived -- 'ensure there are no scrolls on any screen. its essential not to have scrolls' -- addressed as its own root-cause pass rather than an empirical trial-and-error sweep: a genuinely bounded .magic-card-panel (max-height:calc(100vh - 64px); overflow-y:auto; overflow-x:hidden, scoped to awaken mode only) replaced what had been an unbounded panel with nothing for a fit function to measure real overflow against; a real, confirmed layout bug was found and fixed at the root -- .magic-card-awaken-stage's max-width (460px) was narrower than item 6's own enlarged 240px Front/Back cards' combined width (2x240+14=494px), forcing an unnecessary, height-expensive vertical stack via .magic-card-art-row's flex-wrap the moment those bigger cards existed; widened to 510px so the two cards sit side by side as intended. A new _fitAwakenStageToAvailableSpace(panel,stage) -- mirroring the established _fitSkyChallengeToAvailableSpace pattern from the Creator Signature sky-recognition challenge exactly -- runs after every screen builds its own DOM (reveal, claim, nickname, every ceremony beat, and First Claimed Moment) plus from a ResizeObserver on the panel: idempotent, it resets every override first, then escalates through up to four steps only as far as each screen actually needs -- shrink the sky-frame, then the art-canvases, then the ceremony's fixed 240x240 image wrap, then (last resort) solve a --awaken-density CSS custom property via one extra measurement at density 0.5, exploiting that scrollHeight is linear in density -- clamped to a floor of 0.3 so text/spacing never collapses to nothing; a genuinely extreme, smaller-than-any-real-device 320x380 viewport confirmed the escalation correctly bottoms out at that floor and gracefully falls back to the panel's own pre-existing overflow-y:auto safety net rather than breaking anything, exactly the disclosed precedent the sky-challenge's own fit function already established. Verified via a fresh, properly-navigated Playwright sweep across 7 representative viewport sizes (widths 380-1280px, heights 560-900px) driving the full Awakening flow end-to-end at each: zero overflow (scrollHeight===clientHeight exactly) at every single combination, across every one of the five screens, with the First Claimed Moment's Front/Back cards confirmed genuinely side-by-side (not stacked) at every size including the narrowest 380px width. A full regression pass across the existing sky-recognition-challenge/voice suite (unaffected by the shared _renderConstellation sparkle addition -- all scenarios still pass with real, correct behaviour and zero new console errors beyond the same, already-disclosed Story Egg/Nimbus/Quill missing-art and sandboxed-network conditions) and both goldenBuild.js suites (World Builder v1 and v2, unaffected -- this batch touches zero Builder files) passes unchanged. No Theme Contract/Engine V2 Scene Model/Builder/Creator Governing Rules change; confined to js/magicCardUI.js, js/companionDirector.js, js/state.js, css/style.css, and index.html.
